name: Castillo de Peña Cabrera - circular from Diezma through the Parque Natural de la Sierra de Huétor
name_original: Castillo de Peña Cabrera – circular desde Diezma por el Parque Natural de la Sierra de Huétor
region_intro: Mountain biking around Diezma offers a diverse landscape characterized by the Sierra de Huétor Natural Park, featuring varied terrain from forested paths to open areas. The region includes notable natural features such as canyons, reservoirs, and hills, providing a range of elevation changes for mountain bikers. Trails often navigate through natural parkland, offering views of the surrounding environment. Best mountain bike trails around Diezma The most popular mountain bike trail is Cerro del Quejigo — circular desde Tocón, a 15.9 miles (25.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 31 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular path through the local terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Castillo de Peña Cabrera - circular from Diezma through the Parque Natural de la Sierra de Huétor, a moderate 19.9 miles (32.0 km) path. This route explores the natural park, passing by the Castillo de Peña Cabrera. Local mountain bikers also love the Belerda Canyon and Francisco Abellán Reservoir Loop, a 30.2 miles (48.6 km) trail leading through Belerda Canyon and past the Francisco Abellán Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 57 minutes. Mountain biking around Diezma is defined by natural parkland, canyons, and reservoirs. The network offers options for moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Diezma are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Diezma's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Diezma?
A: The mountain bike trails around Diezma are generally rated as moderate or difficult. There are no trails specifically categorized as easy in the komoot database for this area, suggesting that routes require a certain level of experience and fitness.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Diezma?
A: The trails in Diezma are primarily classified as moderate or difficult. Out of 22 available routes, 10 are moderate and 12 are difficult, offering a challenging experience for most mountain bikers.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Diezma?
A: Yes, many of the routes in Diezma are circular. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013574581/cerro-del-quejigo-circular-desde-tocon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Quejigo — circular desde Tocón&lt;/a&gt; is a 25.5 km loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1155660504/castillo-de-pena-cabrera-circular-from-diezma-through-the-parque-natural-de-la-sierra-de-huetor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Peña Cabrera - circular from Diezma through the Parque Natural de la Sierra de Huétor&lt;/a&gt; offers a 32.0 km circular path.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Diezma?
A: You can expect a diverse landscape characterized by the Sierra de Huétor Natural Park. Trails often navigate through natural parkland, featuring varied terrain from forested paths to open areas, canyons, and reservoirs. The region includes significant elevation changes.

Q: What are some longer mountain bike trails in the Diezma area?
A: For a longer ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1150684676/belerda-canyon-and-francisco-abellan-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belerda Canyon and Francisco Abellán Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 48.6 km long and typically takes around 4 hours. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17238667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Gato Viewpoint – Cat's Cave loop from Prado Negro&lt;/a&gt;, covering 45.3 km.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the mountain bike trails in Diezma?
A: Yes, the region offers several scenic viewpoints. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6359134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;End of the World Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/770803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Badlands de Purullena Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17238667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Gato Viewpoint – Cat's Cave loop from Prado Negro&lt;/a&gt; also offers great vistas.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Diezma?
A: The mountain bike trails in Diezma are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. Many riders appreciate the varied terrain within the natural park and the challenging routes available.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while mountain biking near Diezma?
A: The trails often pass through the Sierra de Huétor Natural Park. You can encounter natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4101927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granada Geopark&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3481866" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguas Blancas Spring&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5300006" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Francisco Abellán Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable feature along some routes.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by waterfalls in the Diezma area?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38161443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calialfaqui Waterfall – Alfaguara Camp loop from Prado Negro&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 41.4 km mountain bike trail that includes the Calialfaqui Waterfall.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Diezma?
A: Given Diezma's location in Andalusia, spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler with potential for rain or snow at higher elevations, especially within the Sierra de Huétor Natural Park.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Diezma?
A: While specific parking areas for each trail are not detailed, routes often start from villages like Diezma or Tocón. Look for designated parking in these areas or near natural park entrances. Always ensure you park responsibly and legally, especially within natural park boundaries.

Q: Are there any facilities or recreation areas along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4409627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Sotillo Recreation Area and the Cueva del Agua Interpretation Center&lt;/a&gt;, which can serve as a point of interest or a place to rest during your ride.
tips: Diezma is a small town in Granada, also known as "El mirador de Sierra Nevada". Its main attractions include the church of Nuestra Señora de la Anunciación and the remains of the Castillo de Diezma or Peña Cabrera, an old fortress.
Beautiful views of the Sierra de Huétor Natural Park along the path between Cerro Trincadero and Cerro Picón.

Mentioned in the historical records of the last Zirid monarch of Granada, the Peña Cabrera fortress was erected in the 11th century as a defense against invaders from Almería. It stands on top of a rocky hill and, although the remains are scarce, the point where the castle stood offers a beautiful panoramic view and a visit to this place is a unique opportunity to discover Granada's past and immerse yourself in its history.

waypoints.name: View of Parque Natural Sierra de Huétor
